desc: Heavily filtered, low-fidelity kick/snare chopped straight from a vinyl-sourced loop, all top-end rolled off.
luna: Vinyl (crackle + wear on the drum bus). Pultec HLF-3C (roll off above 8kHz). Studer A800 (2% wow/flutter).
reaper: Vinyl VST on the drum bus -> ReaEQ (steep LPF 7-8kHz) -> CHOWTapeModel (warm saturation, slight wobble).
notes: Chop from a single sample source, not separate one-shots — the loop's own bleed and imperfect timing IS the sound.

desc: A dissonant, slightly detuned piano/strings sample loop — the defining RZA texture.
luna: Manley Massive Passive (scoop 800Hz-1.2kHz to sound thin/lo-fi). Oxide Tape (heavy wow/flutter).
reaper: Kontakt 8 (piano) pitched -15 to -30 cents -> ReaEQ (cut low-mids) -> Tape Cassette 2 (wobble).
notes: Detune by ear until it sits 'wrong' but musical. Leave sample noise/hiss in — don't clean it up.

desc: Layered, shouted ad-libs and posse chants sitting behind the lead verse.
luna: 1176 Rev A (fast attack, hard limiting). Capitol Chambers (short slap room).
reaper: 1176AE VST (fast/fast setting) -> Valhalla Room -> automate volume down under the lead verse.
notes: Record 3-4 different voices/takes shouting the same ad-lib and stack them wide.

desc: A deep, mono, distorted 808/kick foundation under the chopped drums.
luna: bx_boom (sub generator, tuned to root note). bx_blackdist2 (light saturation for grit).
reaper: bx_boom VST -> ParametricOD (light drive) -> mono-sum with Utility.
notes: Keep it mono and centered — it should be felt more than heard next to the chopped drum loop.

desc: The deep, sustained, almost-melodic 808 kick that IS the bassline in Miami bass.
luna: bx_boom (long decay, tuned to root). Manley Variable Mu (gentle glue).
reaper: bx_boom VST (decay maxed) -> TDR Kotelnikov (light bus glue).
notes: Tune the 808 to the actual bassline notes — it functions as the bass instrument, not just a drum.

desc: Rapid-fire 808 clap rolls and hi-hat rolls driving the energy into the drop.
luna: SSL G-Bus Compressor (fast pump on rolls). Pultec EQP-1A (boost 10kHz air).
reaper: SSL G-Bus Compressor VST (fast attack/release) -> ReaEQ (+3dB 10kHz shelf).
notes: Program rolls with increasing velocity into the drop for that classic 'revving' effect.

desc: Shouted, group call-and-response hooks meant for a PA system and a crowd.
luna: 610-A Tube Preamp (drive hot). API 2500 (fast, aggressive glue on the group vocal bus).
reaper: API 2500 VST (4:1, fast attack) -> ReaEQ (boost 2-4kHz for shout presence).
notes: Track it loud and slightly distorted at the source — the grit is the point, don't clean it up in the mix.

desc: Bright, punchy synth stabs and arpeggios in the Latin freestyle/electro tradition.
luna: Minimoog (bright saw). Waterfall Rotary Speaker (subtle movement). Ampex ATR-102 (slap delay character).
reaper: Vital (saw/square unison) -> Kilohearts kHs Delay (1/8 slap) -> Kilohearts kHs Chorus.
notes: Short, staccato stabs on the off-beats. Bright and slightly harsh is correct for this era.

desc: The classic 808 cowbell, clap, and open hat pattern that defines early electro-rap.
luna: dbx 160 (light glue on drum bus). Pultec HLF-3C (roll top end for warmth).
reaper: ReaComp (light glue) -> ReaEQ (gentle LPF ~12kHz).
notes: Program it straight and mechanical — this era is about the machine feel, not human swing.

desc: A vocoded, robotic lead vocal line in the Egyptian Lover / Mantronix tradition.
luna: Auburn Sounds Graillon 3 (vocoder/robot mode). Manley Preamp (warmth going in).
reaper: Graillon 3 VST3 (pitch-tracked robot mode) -> ReaEQ (boost 2-3kHz for intelligibility).
notes: Feed it a simple, monophonic melodic phrase — complex chords get muddy through a vocoder.

desc: A simple, rolling, monophonic 808 or synth-bass pattern under the beat.
luna: bx_boom (short-medium decay). SPL Free Ranger (subtle low-end EQ shaping).
reaper: bx_boom VST -> ReaEQ (HPF at 30Hz to keep it clean).
notes: Keep the pattern simple and repetitive — 2-4 notes looped is authentic to the era.

desc: Multiple layered 808 claps for a wide, snappy backbeat.
luna: Pultec MEQ-5 (boost 3-5kHz presence). Studio D Chorus (subtle width).
reaper: ReaEQ (presence boost) -> Kilohearts kHs Stereo (slight width increase).
notes: Layer 2-3 clap samples at slightly different pitches/timings for width without a chorus effect.

desc: A dark, pitched-down, VHS-tape-quality drum loop sampled from horror soundtracks or old funk breaks.
luna: Oxide Tape (heavy wobble). Pultec HLF-3C (roll off highs hard).
reaper: Pitch sample down 3-5 semitones -> CHOWTapeModel (heavy wobble) -> ReaEQ (LPF 6kHz).
notes: Slow the whole loop 5-10 BPM below the sample's natural tempo for that dragging, sinister feel.

desc: An eerie, minor-key piano or choir stab, often reversed at the tail for a rising swell.
luna: Manley Massive Passive (cut mids for a hollow tone). Hitsville Chambers (dark, short reverb).
reaper: Kontakt 8 (choir/piano) -> ReaEQ (mid scoop) -> Valhalla Room (dark preset).
notes: Reverse a copy of the stab and crossfade it into the front of the hit for a rising swell before impact.

desc: The Three 6 signature — the same vocal line pitched up for hooks and pitched down for ad-libs.
luna: Auburn Sounds Graillon 3 (formant + pitch, two instances at opposite settings). Fairchild 660 on the low layer.
reaper: Graillon 3 VST3 x2 (one pitched up ~5st, one down ~5st with formant lowered) -> 1176 on the down-pitched layer.
notes: Keep the down-pitched 'demon' layer drier and more compressed than the up-pitched hook layer.

desc: A long, sustained, distorted sub bass drone running under the whole beat.
luna: bx_boom (max decay/sustain). bx_yellowdrive (light tube grit).
reaper: bx_boom VST -> ParametricOD (subtle drive) -> LPF at 100Hz.
notes: Let it ring through chord changes rather than retriggering — the constant low drone builds dread.

desc: A rubbery, bouncing synth bass line that drives the whole Bay Area sound.
luna: Minimoog (square/saw blend). Waterfall Rotary Speaker (subtle movement). LA-3A (glue).
reaper: Surge XT (bass patch, slight portamento) -> LA-3A VST (moderate ratio) -> ReaEQ (boost 80-120Hz).
notes: Add a short slide/glide between notes for the signature 'rubber band' bounce.

desc: Fast, syncopated hi-hat and rim patterns pushing the tempo forward.
luna: SSL E-Channel Strip (light compression). Pultec EQP-1A (air boost).
reaper: ReaComp (fast, light) -> ReaEQ (+2dB shelf at 10kHz).
notes: Program hats with alternating open/closed accents rather than a straight 16th pattern.

desc: Shouted, slang-heavy hooks meant to be chanted back at a show.
luna: Neve 1073 (drive the preamp gain hot). 1176 Rev A (fast catch on shouted peaks).
reaper: CHANNEV VST (1073 + 1176 combined) -> ReaEQ (presence boost 3kHz).
notes: Track multiple vocalists shouting the hook together in one pass for authentic crew energy.

desc: A bright, simple synth whistle or flute melody carrying the hook.
luna: Pure sine/triangle wave. Ampex ATR-102 (tape delay character). Ocean Way Studios Deluxe (plate reverb).
reaper: Vital (triangle/sine) -> Kilohearts kHs Delay (dotted 8th) -> Valhalla Room.
notes: Keep it a simple, singable, repetitive 4-8 note melody — it's the most important hook element in the mix.

desc: A warm, walking upright bass sample loop, often slightly swung.
luna: 610-A Tube Preamp (warmth). Fairchild 670 (gentle glue). Capitol Chambers (subtle room).
reaper: ReaEQ (boost 100-200Hz warmth) -> ReaComp (gentle 2:1) -> Valhalla Room (short).
notes: Chop the loop at the bar rather than the beat to preserve the natural swing/bounce of the original playing.

desc: Warm electric piano or jazz guitar chord stabs, sampled and looped.
luna: Neve 1073 (warm boost around 200Hz and 3kHz). Studio D Chorus (subtle width). Lexicon 224 (reverb).
reaper: Kontakt 8 (Rhodes) -> ReaEQ (warmth boost) -> Valhalla Room (Lexicon-style plate).
notes: Keep chords sustained and let them breathe — this style relies on space between hits, not density.

desc: A relaxed, human-feeling drum loop with natural swing, not quantized-tight.
luna: SSL E-Channel Strip (light glue). Pultec HLF-3C (soft top-end roll).
reaper: ReaComp (2:1, slow attack) -> ReaEQ (gentle LPF).
notes: If programming rather than sampling, manually nudge snare hits +/-10-15ms off grid for human feel.

desc: Laid-back, mid-volume, conversational vocal delivery typical of jazz-rap.
luna: Manley Preamp (warm gain). LA-2A (slow, musical compression). Short plate reverb.
reaper: 1073 VST -> LA-2A VST (2:1, slow) -> Valhalla Room (short plate, low mix).
notes: Keep the vocal sitting IN the track, not on top of it — this genre favors blend over dominance.
